customer_anxiety_profile: Zachary is evaluative, not hostile. He's been through a prior solar pitch two years ago and regrets not acting — but that regret makes him a comparison-shopper, not a ready buyer. He wants to understand the current program before committing. He has a specific boundary around credit pulls ('I don't want it run until I'm ready') that signals he wants to control the pace of commitment.
unresolved_concerns: Tax credit availability — Zachary raised this at 01:29 and Meg's answer was incomplete. He may re-raise it with the closer.
Program information email — Meg promised to send 'basics of the program.' If that email doesn't arrive or doesn't satisfy him, it becomes a reason to skip Monday.
Work schedule conflict — Zachary said he'd need to check his schedule before confirming Monday at 3:30. The appointment is not firm.
sensitive_topics_raised: Credit pull boundary — Zachary explicitly said he does not want his credit run until he is ready. Do not frame the credit pull as automatic or routine.
Prior solar pitch two years ago — Zachary has context from a prior company. He will be comparing Ion's offer against that prior experience.
exact_promises_rep_made: Will send an email with 'basics of the program' before Monday
Will call Monday morning to confirm the 3:30 appointment still works
closer_should_know: The appointment is tentative by Zachary's own word and conditioned on his work schedule. The credit-pull boundary is the single most important thing to handle carefully — Zachary flagged it explicitly and will read any pressure on that point as a trust violation. Frame the credit pull as something he initiates when he's ready, not something Ion runs as a step.
